title: Fallen Land 2nd Edition - Top 10 Revisit - Why is it Great?
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8pRl03u-hjI

---

Daniel (@dungeondive) revisits Fallen Land 2nd Edition, a post-apocalyptic sandbox board game that he considers a masterpiece. He highlights several reasons why this game has earned a permanent spot on his top 10:

**Quick Setup, Fun Prep**: The game features speedy initial setup with just board folding and token distribution, followed by the enjoyable character and gear selection phase that keeps the experience engaging.

**Incredible Versatility**: The game supports solo, cooperative, and competitive play equally well—something many titles struggle with. A full scenario book provides dozens of ways to play, including linked campaigns, oneoff missions, and custom solo variants.

**Fantastic Loot and Encounters**: Multiple encounter decks (plains, mountains, cities, roads) combined with a massive, thematic loot deck create an explosion of content. Drawing loot cards never gets old, and the variety ensures fresh experiences every playthrough.

**Gold Standard Documentation**: The rulebook, quick start guide, and player aids are among the best ever made. The detailed turn sequence walkthrough makes learning and referencing rules effortless. Designers should study this as the gold standard.

**Rich Character and Theme Options**: Twenty-four factions, each with unique characters and synergies, provide enormous replayability. The post-apocalyptic theme with a Mad Max vibe is refreshing in a space with limited options.

Daniel notes that bookkeeping exists but is manageable, and recommends his character sheet (available on BGG) to track everything. The small text on some cards might require a magnifying glass in dim lighting, but it's a minor quibble. At $120 for the core game plus Descendants expansion, he calls this an incredible value for such an epic, fiddly sandbox experience.
